In this video, we'll show you the Top 10 Things to do in Valletta, Malta in 2026. This is a travel guide about the best places to visit in Valletta, Malta in 2026.

1:07 #10 Explore the Valletta Waterfront
2:00 #9 Visit MUŻA (National Museum of Fine Arts)

4:19 #7 Manoel Theater: One of the Oldest Theaters in Europe

videos.transcript: the letter is a distinguished city with a legendary past the UNESCO listed capital of Malta stands proudly on one of Europe's grandest Harbors overlooking the Mediterranean Sea and surrounded by some of the mightiest fortifications in the world the city's history is tied to the Knights of Saint John of Jerusalem an 11th century order that provided shelter for pilgrims in the holy land and took on a military role to defend Christianity in 1530 the Spanish King Charles V gave Malta to the knights once Valetta became the new military Fortress of the Order of Saint John the knights set out to create a capital city equal to Europe's finest in this video we're going to be taking a look at the top 10 rated attractions and things to do in Valletta and just wait till you see the number one that we're going to be showing in this video something you would never even have thought of so make sure you watch till the end now before we begin make sure to subscribe to our YouTube channel for more awesome travel guides and make sure to hit them notification Bell so that you know when we publish a new video so if you're ready let's cut to the chase at number 10 explore the Valletta Waterfront overlooked by many tourists the valletto Waterfront is a popular spot among locals this is one of the best places to visit in Valletta for an atmospheric meal by the harbor the row of Baroque buildings along the marsamset harbor was commissioned in the 18th century by Grand Master Manuel Pinto de Fonseca he sounds Grand the buildings were once used as warehouses to store Goods unloaded at the Docks the different colors of the doors indicated the types of contents stored inside for example yellow represented wheat and blue was for seafood well that seems pretty logical the warehouse buildings have been beautifully restored and the pathway has been enlarged fringed with Shady trees and leafy Palms the spacious Waterside Promenade is lined with trendy restaurants at nine visit Musa National Museum of Fine Arts malta's National Museum of Fine Arts is housed in a majestic Palace the Albert Vitali that was originally a residence for the Knights of malter the Monumental staircase at the entrance makes a grand impression worthy of valletta's finest art collection the Museum's collection represents imported works of Maltese art from the 12th century to the 20th century as well as noteworthy Pieces by other European artists there is an excellent assortment of works by the famous artist of Malta matiapretti who was born in Calabria in southern Italy his most renowned work on display here is the baptism of Christ painting the museum also displays a few masterpieces by Italian artist Guido Reni and the exceptional Judith and Halford Ness painting by Valentin de baloin another Exquisite painting that stands out in the collection is the early impressionist view of the Grand Harbor by jmw Turner foreign Collegiate Parish church of Saint Paul's shipwreck an important landmark in Valletta the domed church of Saint Paul's shipwreck is one of the oldest churches in Malta dating back to the 1570s the church is devoted to the father of Christianity in Malta the Apostle Saint Paul as befits a church dedicated to malta's patron saint the interior is Sumptuous and houses venerated relics Pope Pierce VII donated the most precious Relic the block upon which Saint Paul was said to have been beheaded as well as what is believed to be part of his wrist bone visitors should take time to admire the church's sealing frescoes which depict St Paul's brief sojourn in Malta and were painted at the turn of the century the main Altarpiece displays a painting of Saint Paul and Saint Luke in a scene of the Shipwreck this work was painted by Matteo Perez d'alessio in 1580. the church's marvelous gilded wooden statue of Saint Paul is paraded through the streets of Valletta on February 10th every year this date commemorates the day Saint Paul's shipwreck occurred and is celebrated with much festivity foreign Manuel theater one of the oldest theaters in Europe This Magnificent Little Theater was commissioned in 1731 by Antonio Manuel de villena Grand Master of the Knights of Malta to meet the local demand for operas pageants and theatrical performances with this distinguished Heritage the Manuel theater ranks among the oldest theaters in Europe I mean 1731 the lavishly decorated Auditorium features gilded box seats and plush velvet chairs every seat in the house offers a good view and the Acoustics of the oval room are excellent visitors may take a guided tour or a self-guided tour with an audio guide throughout the year the Manuel theater hosts performances including music concerts and classic Opera for tourists one of the most delightful things to do in Valletta is attend an evening performance in this Sensational historic setting in January the Manuel theater hosts a two-week Baroque Festival featuring the music of Vivaldi and Bach next up at 6 Casa Rocka Piccola an aristocratic Maltese home for a unique insight into malta's Heritage tourists can visit the ancestral home of an aristocratic Maltese family who still lives here just past the grand Masters Palace this 16th century Palace was built for Don Pietro La Roca one of the Knights of Malta the current owners are the ninth Marquis and Martinez de Pudo visitors can take a guided tour to see the palace's elegant interior including two dining rooms a bedroom the salons and the small family Chapel one of the rooms displays a family tree that traces their Noble lineage back several Generations the rooms are decorated with antique 16th Century Furniture Moreno glass chandeliers imported from Venice and crystal chandeliers from Bohemia in the salagrande there is a distinctive portable chapel on wheels maybe a cabinet crafted from black lacquer with an altar inside that was designed for personal devotion the palace features a Charming Courtyard Garden typical of Mediterranean architecture also within the property is an inviting restaurant lagiada which serves authentic Regional Sicilian Cuisine the restaurant is open for lunch and dinner several of the palace bedrooms have been renovated for use as bed and breakfast accommodations breakfast is served on the Courtyard Gardens Sunny Terrace or in a cozy room that looks out onto the courtyard and now next up at five upper Baraka Gardens and Military ceremonies the picturesque upper Baraka Gardens encircle part of the city's old fortifications built on valletta's highest point the peaceful and Shady Gardens are beautifully laid out with fountains flower beds rows of archways and statues of prominent historical figures including Winston Churchill there are multiple viewpoints from different outlooks around the garden offering all inspiring panoramas of the Grand Harbor it's possible to see across to the towns of vittoriosa conspicua and Calcutta the level below the gardens is the saluting battery a former Bastion which is lined with military cannons this space is now used for outdoor weddings and ceremonial events without the Cannons a traditional gun salute ceremony takes place every day at noon on the saluting battery performed by members of the malta Heritage Society dressed in authentic British artillery uniforms a tribute to valetta's military history this ceremony includes firing the Cannons as a salute tips for tourists the upper Baraka Gardens is next to Castile place to arrive here go to the Victoria gate and turn left past the church of Saint Mary of Jesus onto Saint Ursula Street the entrance to the upper Baraka Gardens is at the end of the street there is also an elevator at the foot of Saint Barbara Bastion that goes up 60 meters to the upper Baraka Gardens at 4 national museum of archeology the national museum of archeology is housed in the former oberge de Provence the historic residence of the knights originating from the provence region in France the building dates to 1571 and is a wonderful example of Baroque architecture the Grand salon is especially noteworthy with its richly painted walls and wood-beam ceiling the Museum's exceptional archeology collection tells the story of Malta through a chronological presentation exhibits include prehistoric artifacts and Architectural elements found at megalithic sites as well as ancient Roman objects and medieval Antiquities the museum is renowned for its prehistoric collection an extensive array of artifacts from The Maltese Islands megalithic temples these Neolithic era archaeological finds include six thousand to seven thousand year old Pottery ornaments altars Limestone statuettes terracotta bowls and cult utensils the time periods represented by the collection span the prehistoric era continuing until the Phoenician period of antiquity the 4th Century BC a noteworthy piece from The Phoenician Gallery is the bronze Horus and Anubis pendant this pendant features the Egyptian gods believed to deliver Divine assistance in the afterlife one room contains models of the five best preserved temples The Headless fat statues of the toxian temples are displayed here a small room houses the Sleeping Lady considered a masterpiece of prehistoric art this tiny figurine of a woman lying on a couch reveals the expression and emotion of centuries ago an expression from centuries ago fascinating foreign cruise around the Grand Harbor owes much of its military success to its strategic location on the Grand Harbor the enormous Harbor could dock its battleships and still keep Invaders out by closing off the entrance the entire Harbor is surrounded by Massive fortifications burstians and defensive Towers this formidable setting was the scene of the great Siege of 1565 the most famous event in malta's history led by Grandmaster Jean de valet the knights heroically defeated the ruthless ottoman Turk Invaders today the Grand Harbor allows entry of large commercial ships ocean liners and cruise ships the harbor branches off into smaller Creeks shaped like a multi-pronged fork in these small ports there are many yacht marinas and boat docks these ports surround the Valletta Grand Harbor provide enough docks for thousands of boats making it one of the largest marinas in the world the entire Grand Harbor area encompasses a semi-circle of densely populated little towns including calcara vittoriosa conspicua senglia Paola and Marsa just across from Valletta the towns of vitoriosa conspicua and senglia known as the three cities were the original settlement of the knights these towns are filled with historical monuments such as oberge of the knights beautiful Baroque churches and important military forts the best way for tourists to discover the Grand Harbor is by taking a guided boat tour that includes commentary about the sites boat cruises depart from suliama a Seaside Town located across the masamshire Harbor from Valletta a short bus or drive away foreign Masters Palace and Armory valletta's most Monumental building the grand Masters Palace stands in the center of Palace Square which is often used for traditional ceremonies Grandmaster for our Pietro Del Monte commissioned the palace in the 16th century as a residence for the Knights of Malta part of the building is now used as a presidential Palace serving as the office for the president of Malta the rest of the building is open to the public as a museum tourists enter through an impressive Courtyard which leads upstairs to grandiose hallways with lavish 18th century Baroque ceiling paintings the enormous Palace State rooms are richly decorated with gilded moldings and works of art such as a series of murals that illustrates a complete narration of the great Siege of 1565. the Hall of the ambassadors also known as the Red Room is adorned with portraits of the grand Masters who led the knights to Victory one of the paintings depicts the dignified looking Grandmaster Jean de valet founder of the city of Valletta the palace Armory displays the knights armor and weapons swords bows firearms and cannons dating from the 15th century to the 18th century this collection reveals that the Knights of Malta were not ordinary soldiers they were aristocratic Warriors equipped with the most elaborate armor and weapons that money could buy some of the exhibits allow visitors to feel the actual weight of the helmets and finally to even top that at number one Saint John's Co-Cathedral St John's Co-Cathedral reflects the wealth and importance of the Knights of Malta who protected Europe and the Catholic faith from attacks by the ottoman Turks in the 16th century this Cathedral with its opulent flamboyant Baroque interior was built as a symbol of the night's success the knave features opulently decorated ostentatiously gilded walls and gorgeous ceiling paintings by matya pretty malta's most famous painter the most renowned artwork in the church is the beheading of Saint John the Baptist by Caravaggio in the oratory the various chapels of Saint John's Co-Cathedral reflect the different lungs regions of the Knights of Malta who hailed for many different countries in Europe among the eight chapels are a few must-see highlights the Chapel of the long de Aragon the region of Catalonia and navare in Spain features matiapretti's painting of Saint George on a white stallion after slaying a dragon the Chapel of the long of Italy displays the famous work of Saint Jerome writing by Caravaggio after visiting Saint John's Co-Cathedral and surely you'd remember it all tourists can walk down Republic Street to explore the area around Palace Square on the way to the next attraction the grand Masters Palace this area has many quaint shops cafes and restaurants a great place to stop is the famous establishment Cafe Cordina 244 Republic Street a fancy cafe that serves traditional Maltese pastries along with coffee and tea and there you have the top 10 rated attractions and things to do in Valletta did you like what you saw let us know in the comments down below share this video with your friends and don't forget to subscribe to our channel for more fantastic travel guides that's all for now until next time

videos.description:
Top 10 Things to do in Valletta

In this video, I’ll show you the Top 10 Things to do in Valletta. The capital of Malta and one of the smallest, but also most picturesque capital cities in Europe, Valletta is a truly special city to visit that due to it’s sheer beauty and historical sites is itself a Unesco World Heritage site and despite it’s size is packed with lots of things to do. So I’ll help you know how to make the most of your trip here.

🔴 TOP 10 Things to do in MALTA - https://youtu.be/-Q0cQR1GL3Q
🌍 Google Maps Top Things to do in Valletta - https://www.google.com/maps/placelists/list/ZJ07UmsySZKWNkGLWtTkdg

Intro - 0:00
1 St John’s Co-Cathedral - 0:30
2 Upper Barrakka Gardens - 1:08
3 Saluting Battery - 1:38
4 Fort St Elmo - 2:13
5 Streets of Valletta - 2:47
6 National Museum of Archaeology - 3:22
7 Grand Master’s Palace - 4:07
8 Teatro Manoel - 4:42
9 Grand Harbour - 5:25
10 Take a Ferry - 5:52
Outro - 6:27

videos.title: Valletta, Malta | Top 25 Things to Do & See in Valletta | 4K
videos.description:
Valletta is a magically beautiful city and a UNESCO World Heritage Site located on the northeast coast of the island of Malta. It was founded in 1566 by the Grandmaster of the Knights of the Order of St. John as a fortified city. Valletta is the southernmost capital of Europe, with one of the most concentrated historic areas in the world.
Considering the city is very small, with an area of approximately 1 kilometre by 600 metres, the best way to visit all the landmarks is definitely on foot. 
Strolling through the city, you will admire the baroque architecture, historical buildings, picturesque streets and buildings with the distinctive traditional Maltese balconies. 
You will enjoy the panoramic views of the city and the Mediterranean Sea from the fortified city walls. Also, you will see some of the finest artworks, churches and palaces.
One day will be enough for you to visit the most important sights and enjoy the magical atmosphere that the city exudes.

01:25 Here is our list of the top 25 things to do and see in Valletta:

01:30 - 1. Republic Street
02:02 - 2. The National Museum of Archaeology
02:18 - 3. St. John's Co-Cathedral & Museum
02:54 - 4. St. George's Square
03:16 - 5. Grand Master's Palace
03:33 - 6. Caffè Cordina
03:53 - 7. National Library of Malta
04:12 - 8. Independence Square
04:31 - 9. Pjazza Teatru Rjal
04:46 - 10. Palazzo Ferreria
05:06 - 11. Upper Barrakka Gardens
05:57 - 12. The Saluting Battery
06:15 - 13. Castille Square
06:31 - 14. Bastion of Saint James
06:54 - 15. Auberge de Castille
07:12 - 16. Church of Our Lady of Victories  
07:36 - 17. Church of Saint Catherine of Italy
07:49 - 18. Monument to Jean de Valette
08:05 - 19. Merchants Street 
08:32 - 20. MUŻA – The Malta National Community Art Museum
08:49 - 21. City Gate
09:04 - 22. Tritons' Fountain (Triton Fountain)
09:24 - 23. Lower Barrakka Gardens
09:48 - 24. Victoria Gate 
10:08 - 25. Valletta Waterfront

videos.transcript: [Music] Valleta is a magically beautiful city and a UNESCO World Heritage site located on the northeast coast of the island of Malta. It was founded in 1566 by the Grandmaster of the Knights of the Order of St. John as a fortified city. Leta is the southernmost capital of Europe with one of the most concentrated historic areas in the world. Considering the city is very small with an area of approximately 1 km by 600 m, the best way to visit all the landmarks is definitely on foot. Strolling through the city, you will admire the Barack architecture, historical buildings, picturesque streets and buildings with the distinctive traditional Maltese balconies. You will enjoy the panoramic views of the city and the Mediterranean Sea from the fortified city walls. Also, you will see some of the finest artworks, churches, and palaces. One day will be enough for you to visit the most important sites and enjoy the magical atmosphere that the city exudes. Here is our list of the 25 best things to do and see in Valleta. Republic Street is Valleta's main street about 1 kilometer long stretching from Triton's Fountain and the city gates to Fort St. Elmo. Several important buildings, squares, and landmarks are located along and around the street, making it easy to explore because it is primarily a pedestrian zone. Although Republic Street is always crowded with people, it is one of the best places to walk, enjoy the architecture, and restaurants, and serves as a great starting point for exploring Valleta. The National Museum of Archaeology is housed in a magnificent Barack building on Republic Street. Inside the museum, you can see exhibits from the Neolithic period that provide insight into the cultures of extinct civilizations. The Coathedral of St. John is located in the heart of Valleta and its interior is one of the most beautiful and impressive examples of Barack architecture. It was built between 1573 and 1578 and is dedicated to St. John the Baptist and today it is the most visited landmark in Malta. Inside the cathedral, visitors can see works by the artist Matia Pretty, frescos, a marble floor with tombs, beautiful tapestries, and the most essential masterpiece, Caravajio's the beheading of St. John the Baptist. St. George's Square, also known as Palace Square, dates back to the 17th century and is reached by walking along Republic Street. The square is surrounded by historic buildings, including the Grandmaster's Palace, the Casadic Kanti, the main guard building, and a palace that now houses the police station, as well as cafes and a beautiful fountain. The Grandmaster's Palace was built between the 16th and 18th centuries and is located in St. George's Square. It was initially the palace of the Grandmaster of the Order of St. John and today it houses the office of the president of Malta. Cafe Cordina is a prestigious cafe situated in a historic palace on Republic Square opposite the Queen Victoria monument. Established in 1944 by the Cordina family. This cafe is one of the best places to enjoy coffee, people watch and savor traditional handmade sweets and pastries. The National Library of Malta, also known as the Bibliatheica, is situated in an 18th century neocclassical building on Republic Square. It houses an extensive collection of literary masterpieces, and the most valuable part of the library is the archives of the Order of St. John. Independent Square is situated behind the Manual Theater and adjacent to St. Paul's Church, providing visitors with a restbite from the capital's chaos and bustle. Beautiful buildings of traditional Maltese architecture surround this picturesque square making it a perfect spot for taking photos. Patza Tetru Rajal originally the Royal Opera House is a beautiful open air theater located on Republic Street. It was built in 1866 to a design by architect Edward Middleton Barry. Paladza Ferrieria is a beautiful palace situated adjacent to the open air theater. It was built in the 19th century according to the architect Jeppe Beneavia's project. Today, the palace houses several shops and government offices. The upper Baraka Gardens are located right next to Castile Square at the highest point of Valleta's Bastion. They were built in 1661 as a training ground for the Knights of St. John. The gardens offer a beautiful view of the only natural harbor in the Mediterranean and the three cities. At the very entrance to the upper Baraka Gardens, there is a large fountain surrounded by flowers and benches for resting. There is also a sculpture of Lord Gerald Strickland, the former prime minister of Malta, a bust of Winston Churchill, a two monument dedicated to Sir Thomas Maitelland, commemorative plaques and more. From the upper Baraka Gardens, you can take the elevator from a height of 58 m down to the Grand Harbor and the pier from which boats depart for the three cities. The saluting battery is an artillery battery located on a terrace below the gardens in the bastions of Valleta. For over 500 years, it was used to protect the city from enemy attacks. And today, the firing from the 16th century battery is purely ceremonial. [Music] Castillia Square is one of Valleta's largest squares and is home to many notable buildings. It is located very close to the city gate and the upper Baraka Gardens on the hill above the bastion of St. James. St. James Bastion is situated on one of the highest points in Valleta located to the right of the city gate below Castile Square near Upper Baraka Gardens. It is part of the fortifications the construction of which began in 1566. Designed by Franchesco Laparelli, it is home to the headquarters of the central bank of Malta, Oage Castile is one of the most beautiful buildings on Castile Square, which was built in 1570 as the home of the Knights of St. John. The current building constructed in Barack style dates back to 1740 and now serves as the office of the Prime Minister of Malta. The Church of Our Lady of Victory is located next to Castile Square. It was built to commemorate the victory of the Knights to celebrate the vanquishing of the Ottoman invaders in 1565. Right next to the church is a monument to Sir Paul Bofer who was the prime minister of Malta from 1947 to 1950. St. Catherine Church originally built in 1576 is located opposite the church of our lady of victories. The current facade and porch date to 1713 when the church had been rebuilt. The monument to Gene de Vallet was located next to St. Catherine's church. The statue stands in a place which is just a few meters away from the city's foundation stone which he laid with his own hands in 1566. Merchant Street is situated directly adjacent to St. Catherine's Church and extends to Fort St. Elmo and the National War Museum. The street is lined with numerous imposing houses, palaces, and churches offering a blend of rich history, unique architecture, and a lively atmosphere. The street runs parallel to Republic Street, featuring a variety of shops, both old and new, as well as a market. Malta's National Community Art Museum is situated on Merchant Street and showcases artworks from the 15th century to the early 21st century. The museum is open daily from 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. except on Tuesdays. Citygate is the fifth city gate that Valleta has had in the last 450 years. It was built between 2011 and 2014 and is very different from the previous ones as it is not in the traditional Victorian style. Triton's fountain is located in front of the city gate and next to the bus station. The impressive fountain catches the eye with its three bronze sculptures of Triton's gods of the sea. It serves as an excellent meeting point offering plenty of seating to enjoy the view and take a rest. Lower Baraka Gardens dating back to the 17th century are a short stroll from the upper Baraka Gardens and have fantastic views over Grand Harbor. The gardens feature picturesque pathways adorned with flowers, statues, and fountains offering sweeping views of Fort Rico, Fort St. Angelo and the Sleeping Soldier World War II Memorial. The Victoria Gate dates back to 1884, making it the oldest gate in Valleta. The gate features picturesque archways, an ornate multi coat of arms, and blends perfectly with the surrounding architecture. Passing through the gate downhill leads to the Grand Harbor and Valleta waterfront. Valleta's waterfront is comprised of a row of colorful warehouses situated in the Grand Harbor. Built in 1752 by Grandmaster Pinto, it is a place where the Knights of St. John and other merchants used to unload their wares. Today, these spaces have been transformed into restaurants, shops, and bars, all of which feature outdoor seating with views of the water. This prominard is an ideal place to take a break and enjoy a drink while watching the cruise ship's dock. Just across the water from the Grand Harbor in Valleta are the beautiful three cities which you will see in our upcoming video. [Music] Thank you very much for watching and see you again soon.

videos.description:
Valletta in Malta has been one of our favourite ports to visit in the Mediterranean. In fact we loved it so much we recently went back for a long weekend to see more of what this historical city has to offer. And in this video we’ll show you what you can do both by taking excursions and exploring on foot and why we love it so much.

videos.transcript: [Music] theeta in Malta has been one of our favorite ports to visit in the Mediterranean in fact we loved it so much we recently went back there for a long weekend to see more of what this historical city has to offer and in this video we'll show you what you can do both by taking excursions and exploring on foot and also why we love it so much so let's start with what you can do if you're the sort of cruise passenger that likes to wander and explore and Veta is simply wonderful to explore on foot and our first tip is to get yourself a walking map of the city and these are are free and readily available as you exit the cruise terminal now valetta's City Center and main attractions are located high up on the cliff and whilst you can walk through the Victoria gate and take the very very pretty steps up a far more sedentary way to access the city is to take the Baraka lift this costs €1 for a return journey and deposits you right in the heart of one of valetta's prettiest spots the upper Bara Gardens day and night these beautiful gardens attract many visitors visitors and the views across the Grand Harbor are one of the main reasons why and you can also access the saluting battery from here and at noon and at 400 p.m. every day the Cannons are fired across the harbor front a throwback to a naval tradition where cannons were fired to welcome visiting Naval vessels there is also a small Military Museum here and it's only €3 to visit the battery and the museum as well nearby these Gardens are the government buildings Independent Square and Jean the valette square which features both the national Community Art Museum and the open air Royal opera house it's a short walk to feta's Main Street Republic Street which as well as providing plentiful opportunities for shopping also features malta's Museum of archaeology taking in the Splendor of St John's Co Cathedral is on the wish list of many visitors and its opulent interior is certainly spellbinding you can also explore the Palace of The Grandmaster of the Knights of St John as well as the casara Picola palace with both providing insights into the lifestyles of the order of the knights that built and ran this impressive City for hundreds of years Veta is simply great to wander around and explore and walking the walls is particularly rewarding and provides visually stunning views across the Grand Harbor and also maramet Harbor to the North St Elmo's Fort houses another Military Museum and was a critical fortification during the Great Siege of Malta in 1565 and the lower baraca Gardens can be easily overlooked by visitors but are visually stunning and also provide amazing views over the harbor and if you're feeling hungry doing all this exploring well Veta is filled with restaurants and some really fine bars and cafes but in our opinion the wonderful Street Food Market is something not to be missed and the food and the atmosphere inside is simply wonderful and marveling at the tried to be Fountain by the main city gate is also worth your time it really is impressive and and it's right by the bus station too if you're considering venturing further a field but if you simply don't fancy a walk around the city or hav't much time then the Veta Dockside area could be exactly what you're looking for filled with shops bars and restaurants this is a great spot to sit and chill with the ship right in front of you so what about excursions and trips exploring away from Veta well your cruise provider will surely offer you a range of experiences direct from the cruise port and many of these experiences could take year across the island to explore malta's history and Heritage and these excursions are likely to include trips to Medina malta's original capital city this Hilltop medieval Ward Citadel is immaculately preserved and its architectural features are simply stunning exploring the Maze of beautiful streets shops and restaurants is a real delight and St John's Cathedral truly is a Marvel inside there are gorgeous views across the island from the battlements too and you'll be amazed how sleepy this place is and it's population is only 250 people excursions to malta's ancient temples are also popular and when you see these 5,500 year old structures up close you'll see why older than the pyramids and Stonehenge the temples at hajah Haim and manra are simply a wonder and you can't help thinking about how these Neolithic peoples were able to create such incredible structures that perfectly align with celestial events a boat trip Into the Blue Grotto is highly recommended and this limestone caap is truly spectacular and the 30 minute trip along the stretch of coast is an absolute Joy with your boat Skipper weaving in and out of the various sea caves to Glimpse the turquoise blue Waters and if you're after a money-saving tip all three of these destinations are easily accessed using the frequent and easy to use public buses which can be caught from the Veta bus station right next to the truck Fountain and with bus fairs being only €250 each way this can be a real money saving option for those brave enough to give it a go other popular trips include a boat trip to the Blue Lagoon on the nearby island of Cino and the promise of the opportunity to swim in the warm blue turquoise shallow Waters of the Lagoon is a lore for many but be warned this is a very very popular trip and space to set up camp on the nearby Rocky Shore really is out a premium also be aware that some trips take you to the nearby Crystal Lagoon to swim but to be fair there's more space there to swim other boat trips from Veta can take you to the beautiful island of Goo which really is worth exploring the Island's capital Victoria also has a medieval Citadel like Medina this is simply gorgeous to wander around and explore and it's also really peaceful and sleepy and has a gorgeous Cathedral at its heart goo is also easy to navigate and it has a spectacular Coastline and a trip to goo is certainly worth considering if this isn't your first time to malter again these trips can be taken without booking a cruise line Excursion and the boat trips can be taken from either the Veta or the slma key side and this can be a real money-saving option lastly for a simple and very low cost alternative to exploring the streets of Veta taking the short Ferry Crossing across the Grand Harbor towards the three cities of sangla conspic and victoriosa well this is a very rewarding option and for just a few Euros you could sail across the spectacular Harbor past the super Yachts of the Rich and Famous and into these beautiful cities with the historic fortifications museums and Harborside restaurants and bars and these areas are quieter than Veta but are full of charm with a really chilled atmosphere and the faeries across the harbor are really really frequent so this is a trip that's easy to make and you can do so with confidence without worrying about missing the cruise ship sailing away so Veta is a port with plenty to offer and coupled with mol's 3,000 hours of sunshine every year it's friendly and welcoming people great food excellent wine and Prime historical attractions it really is a five-star Cruise destination so we really hope that this video is helping you plan your trip to Malta either sailing from Veta or sailing into Veta because we know you're going to have a great time when you're here and if you're lucky enough to come here for a longer break you're able to take off all of these wonderful things to do and also enjoy a mol's amazing and vibrant nightlife too and you can see more of these experiences in this video here thanks for watching and we hope you have as much fun in Veta as we did if you like this video why not drop us a comment in the box below and hit the like button if you'd like to see more content like this why not subscribe to our Channel it will help us make more content that will help you have more fun way of travel thanks very much
